aposticha_rubric:
The rite of Easter greeting may be repeated as at Matins.
Priests, if present, greet each other in the altar. Then they stand in front of the iconostasis in a line, holding a Cross, a book of Gospels and icons. Laymen approach the priests in order, and kiss first a sacred object held by a priest, then the priest holding it, and then repeat it with the following priests and laymen. Having reached the end of the line, they join it to be greeted in the same manner. The person approaching greets a stationary person with the usual Paschal greeting and the person standing gives the usual response.
During this time, the Paschal troparion is chanted. It is also possible to start the greeting earlier, immediately after starting to chant the Paschal stichera.
